entities.name: HYV-BASED NEW FARM TECHNOLOGY
entities.description: Technology based on High Yielding Varieties hypothesized to have land saving and labour absorbing effects

entities.name: LAND SAVING
entities.description: The effect of reducing the amount of land required for production

entities.name: LABOUR ABSORBING EFFECTS
entities.description: The effect of increasing the demand for labor in agriculture

entities.name: ADDITIONAL CAPITAL
entities.description: Extra financial resources provided to the farm sector to implement new technology

entities.name: SMALL FARMS
entities.description: Agricultural holdings with limited land area

entities.name: WEST GODAVARI DISTRICT
entities.description: A district in Andhra Pradesh where the study was conducted

entities.name: ANDHRA PRADESH
entities.description: A state in India containing the West Godavari District

entities.name: AGRO-CLIMATIC CONDITIONS
entities.description: Environmental factors that influence the suitability of cropping patterns

entities.name: CROPPING PATTERNS
entities.description: The arrangement and sequence of crops grown on a piece of land

entities.name: CASH CROPS
entities.description: Capital intensive crops grown primarily for sale and profit

entities.name: HIGH YIELDING VARIETIES
entities.description: Crop varieties developed to produce higher yields per unit area

entities.name: RURAL EMPLOYMENT
entities.description: The state of being employed in rural agricultural areas

entities.name: NET PROFITABILITY
entities.description: The final financial gain from crop production after all expenses
